issue_title: [CVE-2022-41881] Denial of Service (DoS) vulnerability in io.netty:netty-codec-haproxy
issue_body:
### Description

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final) › io.netty:[netty-codec-haproxy@4.1.82.Final](mailto:netty-codec-haproxy@4.1.82.Final)

# Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Denial of Service (DoS) due to a stack overflow that can be triggered by a malformed message containing a deeply nested TLV. (The only limitation on such recursion is that the TLV length cannot exceed `0xffff`.)

**NOTE:** The StackOverflowError is caught if HAProxyMessageDecoder is used as part of Netty’s ChannelPipeline.

# Details

Denial of Service (DoS) describes a family of attacks, all aimed at making a system inaccessible to its intended and legitimate users.

Unlike other vulnerabilities, DoS attacks usually do not aim at breaching security. Rather, they are focused on making websites and services unavailable to genuine users resulting in downtime.

One popular Denial of Service vulnerability is DDoS (a Distributed Denial of Service), an attack that attempts to clog network pipes to the system by generating a large volume of traffic from many machines.

When it comes to open source libraries, DoS vulnerabilities allow attackers to trigger such a crash or crippling of the service by using a flaw either in the application code or from the use of open source libraries.

# Remediation

Upgrade io.netty:netty-codec-haproxy to version 4.1.86.Final or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/netty/netty/commit/cd91cf3c99123bd1e53fd6a1de0e3d1922f05bb2)
- [CVE-2022-41881](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2022-41881)
- https://github.com/netty/netty/security/advisories/GHSA-fx2c-96vj-985v
- Related to #16120

issue_title: [CVE-2022-41915] HTTP Response Splitting vulnerability in io.netty:netty-codec
issue_body:
### Description

-   Package Manager: maven
-   Vulnerable module: io.netty:netty-codec
-   Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT, org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT and others

### Detailed paths

-   _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-app@999-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:[quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-vertx@2.13.5.Final) › io.quarkus:[quarkus-netty@2.13.5.Final](mailto:quarkus-netty@2.13.5.Final) › io.netty:[netty-codec@4.1.82.Final](mailto:netty-codec@4.1.82.Final)

# Overview

[io.netty:netty-codec](https://github.com/netty/netty) is an event-driven asynchronous network application framework.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to HTTP Response Splitting when calling `DefaultHttpHeaders.set` on an iterator of values, because header value validation is not performed.

# Workaround

This vulnerability can be worked around by changing the `DefaultHttpHeaders.set(CharSequence, Iterator<?>)` call into a `remove()` call, and calling `add()` in a loop over the iterator.

# Remediation

Upgrade `io.netty:netty-codec` to version 4.1.86.Final or higher.

# References

-   [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/netty/netty/commit/fe18adff1c2b333acb135ab779a3b9ba3295a1c4)
- [CVE-2022-41915](https://www.cve.org/CVERecord?id=CVE-2022-41915)

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

`No files were found with the provided path: **/java_pid*.hprof. No artifacts will be uploaded.` is reported with almost every workflow job run since #16090.
See e.g. https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/3740105489.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

No warning is reported in CI

### Actual behavior

Warning is printed

### How to Reproduce?

Submit a PR and watch the Keycloak CI workflow warnings.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: SSSD User Federation dissapeared in 20.0.1/20.0.2
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

The SSSD User Federation feature, described here (https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_sssd), seems to have been removed in the latest versions of keycloak.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/15587#discussioncomment-4267475.

Was this an accidental removal, or is it intended that this feature be removed. If intentional, I suggest that the documentation be updated.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

When sssd is configured on the keycloak server's host machine, SSSD User Federation should be listed as an option in the User Federation admin page, and it should be possible to use the functionality.

### Actual behavior

The SSSD User Federation option never shows up in the admin page.

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the instructions at https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#_sssd. The SSSD User Federation never shows up.


### Anything else?

Happy to help debug the issue. I'm on libera in the freeipa channel.

issue_title: AccountConsole leaks translated messages into cached theme
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

With the new AccountConsole the the messages bundle containing translated keys is reused when realm localizations are applied.
When the themes are cached, this results in the translated messages being written and cached into the translation bundle, and deletion of translation keys is no longer possible.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Deleted translation keys are respected and not shown anymore.

### Actual behavior

Deleted translation keys are not properly deleted from the cached theme instance.

### How to Reproduce?

Add a translation for a given key:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215426-4f22dd8a-4569-423b-85aa-fdb80c4d1521.png)
Check the translated key in the account console:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215520-54fd5d55-df25-4b9a-8495-f9e1042a1b8f.png)
Delete the translation:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215550-9b950fc1-9d19-435b-b6e9-d3194f2d2e27.png)
Check the key again, the translation still exists there.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1145424/210215580-a90e0962-c61d-4151-b7d4-461e1e3f4065.png)

### Anything else?

The message bundle is loaded and, using `putAll`, updated with all localized texts.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/a12f50f8d99b4eabada5c03a28832aee12098577/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/account/AccountConsole.java#L114-L118
After removing a key, the cached theme instance is not reset.

The theme instance comes from the `CachedThemeManager$ExtendingTheme` `getMessages` method, whenever the theme cache is enabled.

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

### Describe the bug

according to [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/12105) and [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/11938#issuecomment-1125201574) the operator should be watching namespaces defined in the `QUARKUS_OPERATOR_SDK_NAMESPACES` env variable, but it doesn't seem to work for me.

### Version

issue_title: Wrong auth session id being used when validating auth session id cookies
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When validating the auth session cookies to see if they have a corresponding valid authentication session on the server, the code is resolving the id from an iterator and potentially ignoring valid cookies when multiple authentication session cookies are set in the request.

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/0f2ca3bfddeeeece06cae4cbd1f7122adb261eed/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationSessionManager.java#L205.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Should validate each cookie individually.

### Actual behavior

Only validates the first cookie set in the request, randomly.

### How to Reproduce?

See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/16132

### Anything else?

_No response_

Wrong auth session id being used when validating auth session id cookies (#16253)

Closes #16252
Closes #16132

issue_title: Field generator: `getCollectionElementClass` method not generated when no addElement method is present in interface
issue_body: When there is no `addElement(E element)` method in entity interface, the `getCollectionElementClass` is not generated for the field in `Map*EntityFields`. It's caused by the fact that there is `FieldAccessorType.COLLECTION_ADD` used as a condition for adding it: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/10f2ae34113ec86fb80682b08fd4a27b68383db7/model/build-processor/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/map/processor/GenerateEntityImplementationsProcessor.java#L180-L185
commit_message:
Field generator: getCollectionElementClass method not generated when no addElement method is present in interface

Closes #16255

issue_title: Incorrect handling of OptionParserException in kcadm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/cli

### Describe the bug

I ran a `kcadm` command (`config credentials [...]`) with an incorrect flag (`--username` vs `--user`). This raises `OptionParserException`, which results in a misleading error message.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

It should tell me that I put a bad flag or something instead.

### Actual behavior

This returns `Unknown command: credentials`, which is a misleading error.

### How to Reproduce?

Run something like: `/opt/keycloak/bin/kcadm.sh config credentials --server http://localhost:8080 --realm master --username admin --password admin`

(this has the incorrect `--username` flag)

This will return `Unknown command: credentials`, which is a misleading/incorrect error message.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Do not show username field when updating profile if UPDATE_EMAIL feature is enabled and email as username is enabled
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When updating profile, the `username` field should not be available if:

* `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature is enabled, forcing users to update e-mail through a required action.
* `Edit username` is enabled and email as username is also enabled

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

The `username` field only visible when e-mail attribute is readable.

### Actual behavior

The `username` field is shown even though email as username is enabled and `UPDATE_EMAIL` feature is enabled.

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a realm
* Enable User Profile
* Enable Registration
* Enable Email as Username
* Enable Edit Username
* Register a new user
* Enable the `Verify Profile` action to the newly created user
* Try to log-in as the user
* The `username` field is shown even though email as username is enabled

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not show username field when updating profile if UPDATE_EMAIL feature is enabled and email as username is enabled

Closes #16263

issue_title: EntityField `mapPut` and  `collectionAdd` default methods doesn't insert an element when `get(e)` returns `null`
issue_body: `EntityFiled.get(E e)` may return `null` and in that case default method `mapPut` (and `collectionAdd`) do not insert the element into a map (or into a collection).

issue_title: KcSamlSignedBrokerTest failing in FIPS mode
issue_body:
This is follow-up of #14969 . As even after adding the `XMLDSig` provider to java.security providers, the `KcSamlSignedBrokerTest` is failing when Keycloak server running on FIPS.

Reason is, that BCFIPS does not allow same private key to be used for both signing and decryption, which is actually used in `KcSamlSignedBrokerTest` (affected is private `rsa` key in `consumer` realm. As this realm uses private key for signing IDTokens of it's clients, but at the same time, this private key is used to decrypt encrypted SAML assertions sent from SAML IDP (represented by realm `provider`)

issue_title: Service Accounts Client must create the Client ID mapper with Token Claim Name as client_id
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

[Enabling service account](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_service_accounts) create three default mappers.
One of them is 'Client ID' mapper. This mapper has default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to clientId.
Default 'Token Claim Name' field must be **client_id** in order to be compliant with OAuth2 specifications.
OAuth2 specifications related to **client_id** claim:

- [JSON Web Token (JWT) Profile for OAuth 2.0 Access Tokens ](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9068#section-2.2)
- [OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7662#section-2.2)
- [OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8693#section-4.3)

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

 'Client ID' mapper with default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to client_id.

### Actual behavior

 'Client ID' mapper with default 'Token Claim Name' field equal to clientId.

### How to Reproduce?

[Enable service account](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/#_service_accounts) in a Client

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Service Accounts Client must create the Client ID mapper with Token Claim Name as client_id

closes #16329

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Describe the bug

First I apologize for not being able to test this on the newest version. The version tested was 20.0.1 and I think this is still applicable to the newest release as well. I am not able to perform an installation easy in my work environment so I don't have the newest version to test against. 

During testing, we determined that it was possible to create Realm names that had invalid characters and subsequently couldn't be deleted. The simplest way to reproduce this issue is by doing the following steps:

issue_title: Cannot access subgroups with just "view-users" role except in new tab - javascript error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

In the new admin theme, if my user has the 'view-users' role of the realm-management client I am able to see and access the "Users" and "Groups" menu items. I can access them and list groups, but I am not able to click on a subgroup due to error "`r().access is undefined`"
I am however able to list the subgroup contents in a new tab by middle-clicking
I can also list the subgroups if I have the "manage-users" role but that is not an option for me since it allows editing user details.

### Version

20.0.0

### Expected behavior

There should be consistency in subgroup listing. If I can list all groups with the "view-users" role, I should be able to view groups as well as subgroups.

### Actual behavior

Subgroups can only be viewed in new tab due to javascript error related to missing "access" key on subgroups.

`r().access is undefined`

### How to Reproduce?

0. Create realm "Bug"
1. Create a parent group and a child group:
  * Parent
      * Child
2. Create a user and add the 'view-users' role of the realm-management client to it using the role mapping page -> client-roles -> realm-management
3. Log in with the new user in the realm console
4. Click "Groups" - Groups will appear
5. Click the "Parent" group - subgroups will apear
6. Click the "Child" group - you will see error - `r().access is undefined`

### Anything else?

I have investigate requests and it seems when listing groups the first request done is:
`realms/Bug/admin-ui-groups?search=&first=0&max=101&global=false`
This returns a list of groups containing keys including "`subgroups`" and "`access`"
In our case, since we only have the 'view-users' role, the access key contains the following:
~~~ json
{
	"access": {
		"view": true,
		"manage": false,
		"manageMembership": false
	}
}
~~~
However the child group contains no such key:
~~~ json
{
	"0": {
		"id": "245c20d7-5728-4ce3-bfdf-3ddd7b7ac4cf",
		"name": "Child",
		"path": "/Parent/Child",
		"attributes": {},
		"realmRoles": [],
		"clientRoles": {},
		"subGroups": []
	}
}
~~~

Now the weird thing is, if I give the user the role "`manage-users`", the access key of the parent becomes:
~~~ json
{
	"access": {
		"view": true,
		"manage": true,
		"manageMembership": true
	}
}
~~~
And it allows clicking on the Child group although this child still doesn't have the "access" key.
I think `"manage": true` circumvents that bug but it isn't an option for me.

It's also not an option to use older theme, since it lacks the functionality to add users into groups in the group listing entirely.

## Background - fyi
My use case is the following: I want to have users be able to see and browse through groups without being able to edit user details. I will use this functionality with fine-grained permissions in order to have team leaders manage membership of their own groups without letting them edit their users, which is something HR will manage. Setup works. I can add users to subgroups in the users page, but I cannot access the subgroup to add users into it except using "open in new tab"
commit_message:
add fine grain access to subgroups (#16672)

fixes: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-ui/issues/4195

issue_title: Documentation - Expand/Clarify Admin REST API User Search Functionality
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The documentation around queries for user information via the Admin REST API is incomplete/misleading.

### Version

20.0.2

### Expected behavior

Clear explanations of the behaviors and options available given how the system is configured.

### Actual behavior

Documentation does not present a whole/accurate picture of behaviors/options.

### How to Reproduce?

When an AD/LDAP federation is configured and the sync option is enabled user information is queried and cached in the Keycloak database.  After this it appears that on every query of the Admin API for users (ex. GET /{realm}/users) the result is Keycloak issuing an LDAP query *per user* for user attributes, etc.  If the system has many thousands of users this is not desirable behavior.
On the other hand when an AD/LDAP federation is configured and the sync option is *disabled* the user information is never pulled down from AD to the Keycloak database.  In this case when the same query is run against the API no results (or only users created locally) are returned. However the addition of "?username=*" results in *one* LDAP query (in my case for sAMAccountName=*) with the appropriate...based on the DNs and other options configured in the federation setup...result set.  However the use of the wildcard/asterisk is not documented *and* it does not seem to work if the sync option is enabled.

### Anything else?

Keycloak documentation states "The advantage of synchronization is that all Keycloak features work efficiently because any required extra per-user data is stored locally." [link](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#storage-mode).  However issuing thousands of queries to an AD/LDAP server does not seem to be an efficient method of querying when the scope of the query is fairly large.  Providing expanded documentation in this space might allow users to better understand the ramifications of their configuration choices and how to leverage the options (like the wildcard search in an system not sync'ing) available to them.

issue_title: Token introspection response does not follow RFC6479 "scope" parameter format
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Currently, the introspection response include a scope parameter even if no scope is granted. It will be empty, e.g. `"scope": ""`.

Token introspection spec in [RFC6479 section 3.3](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6749#section-3.3) specifies the format of this field is:

     scope       = scope-token *( SP scope-token )
     scope-token = 1*( %x21 / %x23-5B / %x5D-7E )

My understanding is that means "at least one scope-token" and scope-token must include "at least one character" (in those ranges).

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

If no scope is granted, then the "scope" field should not be present.

### Actual behavior

An empty value of `"scope": ""` is included.

### How to Reproduce?

Request a grant with no scopes, and call the token introspection endpoint.

### Anything else?

This behavior is the same as in access tokens per #16523 , however I consider that a separate issue because Keycloak defines its own format of JWTs for access tokens, and this is not specified by OAuth. So  16523 is more of a "nice to have" to be consistent with token introspection (as well as RFC9068).

This can be worked around by requesting at least one scope.
commit_message:
Do not return empty scope field in token introspection response

Closes #16526

commit_message:
Fix pagination issue with H6

With Hibernate ORM 6, pagination started to be unreliable: When
setting the max results only if the first row was 0 has randomly
affected other threads where first row was greater than 0. The
latter thread sometimes produced query which did *not* account
for the offset (cf. threads `-t1` and `-t2` below, while `-t2`
missed the `offset ? rows` part whic `-t3` has).

This has been fixed by setting the first row offset unconditionally.

Closes: #20202
Closes: #16570


issue_title: Setting user password and entering "password confirmation" first leads to blocking of "save"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

account/api

### Describe the bug

Try setting a password for a user and enter the "password confirmation" field first and then "password" afterwards. Even if both password are exactly the same the app says "Password and confirmation does not match." and won't let you save.

Sure it is not the expected workflow to first fill "password confirmation" but in my case a made a typo in the first password field and I went back to correct it just to see that the app doesn't care for my correction.

Please make sure that BOTH fields get the necessary validation listeners assigned to.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Regardless of the order of input both password fields should be checked for match.

### Actual behavior

Only on inputing into the "password confirmation" field leads to check for match.

### How to Reproduce?

Type in the "password confirmation" first and then in the "password" field.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Impossible to update a federated user credential label
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

When an administrator modifies a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is not saved.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

When modifying a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is saved.

### Actual behavior

When modifying a federated user credential's label from the admin UI and click on "Save", the new user label is not saved.

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create a federated user with a credential
2) In the admin UI, search for this user
3) In the credentials tab, modify the credential label and click on save

### Anything else?

The bug comes from org.keycloak.storage.jpa.JpaUserFederatedStorageProvider#updateCredential(org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, java.lang.String, org.keycloak.credential.CredentialModel) : the entity is not updated with the user label coming from the credential model given in parameter, it is the other way around
commit_message:
Impossible to update a federated user credential label
Closes #16613

issue_title: Database migrations are not transactional
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Hey there!

It appears that migrations in Keycloak are not done in a transactional manner - that is, if you launch two or more Keycloaks on an empty database, there is a good chance that one of them will crash because it will try to upgrade the database schema while the first instance is still initializing. This came as a surprise to me - while schema modifications are not transactional in quite a few databases, the usual schema migration tools guard against this by using a "lock table" (e.g. DATABASECHANGELOGLOCK for Liquibase), so I assume this is unintentional.

### Version

20.0.3

### Expected behavior

Keycloak should start - one instance should be doing the schema upgrade, the other instance should block trying to acquire a lock while that is in progress.

which for me looks like the code in `QuarkusJpaUpdaterProvider` is actually not guarded against running concurrently in different instances.

### How to Reproduce?

The easiest way to reproduce this is using docker-compose:
```
mkdir conf
keytool -genkeypair -storepass password -storetype PKCS12 -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -dname "CN=server" -alias server -ext "SAN:c=DNS:localhost,IP:127.0.0.1" -keystore conf/server.keystore
```
Create a file named `docker-compose.yaml` with the following contents:
```
version: '3'

services:
  database:
    container_name: keycloak_db
    image: "postgres:14"
    command: [ "postgres"]
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:5433:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: "keycloak"
      POSTGRES_USER: "postgres"
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "postgres"
    restart: always
  keycloak:
    image: quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:20.0.3
    volumes:
      - ./conf:/opt/keycloak/conf
    command:
      - start
```

commit_message:
Align startup of Quarkus with the regular startup to ensure boostrap locks are created.

Also fixing an issue where DBLockGlobalLockProviderFactory held on to an old session, which lead to a closed DB connection on Quarkus.

Closes #16642
